categories.name: WORDS AT THEIR FINAL RESTING PLACES

questions.clue: "Heroine of the Underground Railroad. Nurse and scout in the Civil War"
This musician: "Satchmo"
"Mother of the modern day civil rights movement"
This OK Corral survivor: "That nothing's so sacred as honor, and nothing so loyal as love!"
This British leader of men in Antarctica: "I hold that a man should strive to the uttermost for his life's set prize"

I: a high-fiber cereal item transforms into an organ you should be using right now
O: a male sheep & the way he might wander around
U: stationery becomes a very poor person
First step in making frose: do this to rose wine
To make a Jagerbomb, drop a shot of this German liquor into Red Bull
RumChata cream liqueur combines rum & this sweet cinnamony drink
Frou-frou drink of vodka, cider & schnapps

While number 2 in the U.S., it's the number one vegetable oil used in Canada & Japan

This once-popular term for a young female hospital volunteer referred to the red & white uniform

(Ken Jennings presents the clue.) One of the first two men to summit Everest & a part of my personal Mount Rushmore of all-time climbers, he also tractored to the South Pole five years later in 1958

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Ken Jennings presents the clue.) In 2015, Dani Arnold climbed this 14,700-foot peak in the Pennine Alps in 1 hour, 46 minutes, but I did Disneyland's in, like, a minute

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Ken Jennings presents the clue.) Though the Freerider route takes most people four days, it took Alex Honnold about 4 hours, ropeless & using some holds the width of a pencil, to climb El Capitan in this national park

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Ken Jennings presents the clue.) Braving subzero temps on June 7, 1913, Walter Harper was the first person to stand atop this 20,310-foot peak; of Athabascan heritage, he was the one Native American member of the expedition & the mountain itself is now known by its indigenous name

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Ken Jennings presents the clue.) Go Canada! In 2018, Monique Richard overcame a fall into a crevice to be the first woman to solo summit this mountain, Canada's tallest

final.clue: It's the largest country in area that begins & ends with the same letter
